description: Snapshot of API routes that are NOT documented in any OpenAPI spec (neither legacy `openapi.json` nor v1 generator output). These are GRANDFATHERED — the coverage gate (L14-01) will not fail if a path in this list stays undocumented, but it WILL fail if a NEW route is added that isn't documented and isn't here. Shrink this list over time; never grow it without rationale.
rationale_l17_01_addendum: Seven routes added below this comment are GRANDFATHERED retroactively as part of the L17-01 PR (withErrorHandler universal financial wrap). They were created by previously-merged fixes (L02-06 withdraw lifecycle, L02-10 chunked clearing, L06-04 cron health, L08-07 wallet drift, L12-04 cron SLA, L01-38 CSP report) that never registered the route in src/lib/openapi/routes/. L17-01 itself did NOT change any request/response schema (drift check is GREEN — public/openapi-v1.json is byte-identical to the registry output) — it only wrapped existing handlers with withErrorHandler. Documenting the seven orphan routes is tracked as a separate follow-up under the L14-01 contract-first registry workstream and will be resolved by registering each in src/lib/openapi/routes/ with a proper Zod schema for the response and removing the entry from this baseline.
rationale_l05_03_addendum: L05-03 itself documents the two new routes /api/distribute-coins/batch and /api/v1/distribute-coins/batch in BOTH legacy public/openapi.json AND v1 public/openapi-v1.json (via src/lib/openapi/routes/v1-financial.ts). The single grandfathered entry added in this PR is /api/platform/health, an orphan route from an earlier in-flight finding (platform observability surface) that L05-03 did NOT touch but that is now flagged because the coverage gate runs end-to-end. Documenting /api/platform/health is tracked under the L14-01 contract-first registry workstream and will be resolved by registering it with a Zod schema and removing the entry from this baseline.
rationale_l05_09_addendum: /api/platform/custody/{groupId}/daily-cap (GET/PATCH) is added in L05-09 (custody daily deposit cap antifraud guardrail). It is a platform-admin-only endpoint that is intentionally NOT on the v1 public surface (no client SDK consumers — only the platform-admin UI calls it via csrfFetch). Documenting it is tracked under the L14-01 contract-first registry workstream as a follow-up and will be resolved by registering it in src/lib/openapi/routes/v1-platform.ts (TODO) with a Zod schema mirroring the route handler. Until then it is grandfathered to keep the coverage gate green; the route ITSELF is fully tested via portal/src/app/api/platform/custody/[groupId]/daily-cap/route.test.ts and tools/test_l05_09_custody_daily_cap.ts.
